"HOMER’S BARBERSHOP QUARTET" by

Jeff Martin

ACT ONE FADE IN: EXT. SPRINGFIELD SWAP MEET - DAY SCENE 1 A banner reads: "Springfield Swap Meet." There is a

drawing of a cornucopia filled with auto parts, old clothes, a TV with a coat hanger for its antenna, etc.. PAN DOWN to DIAMOND JOE QUIMBY, surveying the scene from his podium. MAYOR QUIMBY (TO SELF) Human roaches. Feeding off each other’s garbage. The only thing you can’t buy here is dignity. (TAPS MIKE) (HAPPILY) Welcome swappers to the Springfield Swap meet. Ich Bin Eine Springfield Swap Meet Patron! The audience APPLAUDS MAYOR QUIMBY (CONT’D) (TO SELF GLUMLY) I need a drink and a shower. ANGLE MOE Under a sign "HANDICRAFTS By MOE" (with the "Moe" written

in flowing script), MOE hawks a display of oyster shells with painted-on lipstick and eyelashes.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

MOE Oyster shells, hand painted to resemble Lucille Ball! You’1ll love Oyster Lucy! (MOVES OYSTER SHELL, IMITATING LUCY) Oh Mr. Mooney, I just gotta meet Bob Cummings. Vivvwv!!! ANGLE - FLANDERS

BART, MILHOUSE, and NELSON stop at the FLANDERS’ table, where a sign reads: "FREE TRADING CARDS."

BART Oh boy! Free trading cards!

The boys pick up elaborate Biblical trading cards, which we see.

MILHOUSE Wow, Joseph of Aramathia! (FLIPS IT OVER) Twenty-six conversions in A.D. 46! NELSON Whoa, a Methuselah rookie card! FLANDERS Well, boys, who’d have thought learning about religion could be fun? BART (SPOOKED) Religion? MILHOUSE (SPOOKED) Learning? NELSON

Let’s get outta here!

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92 3.

They run away. FLANDERS Hey! ANGLE ON LISA SCENE 2 At a table with toys, games and dolls. LISA Wow an original Malibu Stacey from 1958. (NOTICING BREASTS) Oh my.

We see a vintage girl doll. The bosoms are unnaturally huge and pointy.

MAN AT TABLE (WISE GUY VOICE) Yeah. They took her off the market after some kid put both his eyes out. LISA Really, how? MAN AT TABLE He tried to kiss it. ANGLE - HERMAN’S TABLE

At HERMAN’S table of Military Souvenirs, PRINCIPAL SKINNER examines an "iron mask," a metal cylinder with an eye slit.

PRINCIPAL SKINNER "Prisoner 24601"... (CHUCKLES) Why, I wore this for two years in a Viet Cong internment center. Never thought I’d see the old girl again.

He slips it on.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

PRINCIPAL SKINNER (CONT’D) (SLIGHT ECHO) Still fits. HERMAN Small world. PRINCIPAL SKINNER (ECHO) It is, it really really is. ANGLE HOMER He's at MRS. GLICK’S table, looking through a big box of stuff labelled "ALL ITEMS $5." He picks up and rejects, in order, an original copy of the Declaration of Independence, a copy of Action Comics Number 1, a sheet of stamps, and a violin. HOMER Junk... junk... the airplane’s upside down... Strad-i-who-vius?...

Then he pulls out an old bucket with the bottom rusted away.

HOMER (CONT’D) Hey, hey!

ANGLE - SIMPSON TABLE SCENE 3 MARGE is talking to the "JUST STAMP THE TICKET" MAN. MARGE Your teenage son or daughter will think this wishbone necklace is

really "cool."

JUST STAMP THE TICKET MAN I doubt my son or daughter is that stupid.

MARGE (MURMUR)

4.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

"JUST STAMP THE TICKET MAN" walks away as Homer runs up with his purchase.

HOMER Hey, Marge, look what I just bought. An old bucket! MARGE (MURMURS) It doesn’t have a bottom. HOMER (MOANS) You’re right. What was I thinking? Worthless piece of junk. Homer throws the bucket away. The bucket lands in the foreground and rolls toward the camera. We see an inscription. "THIS BUCKET PROPERTY OF ABE LINCOLN." ANGLE - COMIC BOOK GUY The COMIC BOOK GUY peels the breading off a corndog and eats it as Bart and LISA check out his table. Bart picks up an album with the cover: "‘S Wonderful, ‘S Marvelous, ‘S Krusty" -- Krusty holding a microphone and wearing a tux. Bart pulls out an album labelled: "Melvin and the Squirrels" featuring three squirrels that resemble human boys in sweaters with their initials on their sweaters. BART What the hell is this? COMIC BOOK GUY (MOUTH FULL) Melvin and the Squirrels. Part of the rodent

invasion of the early 60s.

He sets it on a turntable and we hear three speeded up voices SINGING "Yankee Doodle."

SPEEDED UP VOICES "Stuck a feather in his cap and

called it... "

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

MELVIN (SPEEDED UP) Rice-a-Roni! ANGRY MAN Mel-vin! Lisa picks up another album and GASPS. LISA Bart, look! It’s Dad! CLOSE-UP = ALBUM COVER SCENE 4 It's a record called "BARBERSHOP IS HERE TO STAY!" Homer, wearing a straw boater, is a member of the pictured quartet. BART Dad, when did you record an album? HOMER I’m surprised you don’t remember, son. It was only eight years ago. BART Dad, thanks to television, I can’t remember what happened eight minutes ago. Everyone LAUGHS. BART (CONT’D) No, really, I can’t. It’s a serious problem. Everyone LAUGHS again. Bart joins in the LAUGHTER BART (LAUGHING) What are we all laughing

about?

6

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92 7.

Everyone continues to LAUGH. HOMER Who cares? HOMER Anyway, it all happened during that magical summer of 1985... A maturing Joe Piscopo left "Saturday Night Live" to conquer Hollywood... People Express introduced a generation of hicks to plane travel... and I was in a barbershop quartet... DISSOLVE TO: INT. MOE’S TAVERN - CONTINUOUS

Inside the bar Homer, Skinner, WIGGUM and APU hop up on stage. Wiggum BLOWS on a pitch pipe.

PRINCIPAL SKINNER (SINGING) Hello...

HOMER (SINGING) Hello...

WIGGUM (SINGING) Hello...

APU (SINGING) Hello...

ALL (SINGING) Hello, ma baby/ hello, ma honey/ hello, ma ragtime, ragtime

gal/ Send me a kiss by wire... etc.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92 8.

PAN across the quartet as they SING a nifty barbershop rendition of the old song.

HOMER (V.O.) Every afternoon at Moe’s, Chief Wiggum, Principal Skinner, Apu and I would get together and sing... and the crowds went wild.

As they SING they are pelted with roses and women’s panties.

REVERSE ANGLE We see only BARNEY in the audience. He waves at the group. BARNEY Yoo hoo! DISSOLVE TO: EXT. SWAP MEET - THE PRESENT BART Barbershop! That kind of music hasn’t been popular since grandma had hair. HOMER Rock ‘n’ roll had become stagnant. "Achy Breaky Heart" was seven years away. Something had to fill the void, and that something was barbershop... DISSOLVE TO: MONTAGE SCENE 5

of the quartet singing at various venues.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

1) OLD FOLKS HOME The four are on stage. The OLD FOLKS watch. PRINCIPAL SKINNER (SINGING) Goodbye... HOMER (SINGING) Goodbye... WIGGUM (SINGING) Goodbye... APU (SINGING) Goodbye... ALL (SINGING) Goodbye, my Coney Island Baby. GRAMPA That’s my son up there. OLD MAN What, the balding fat-ass? GRAMPA (LYING) No, the Hindu guy. 2) SPRINGFIELD PENITENTIARY They perform for the PRISONERS. ALL (SINGING) Never to see you any... WIGGUM (SINGING) Never gonna see you any... ANGLE - JAILBIRD

Brandishing rock.

9.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

JAILBIRD I’m gonna nail that cop right between the eyes. Jailbird starts to smile and sway to the music. JAILBIRD (CONT’D) (SOFTENING) Right after this song is over. DISSOLVE TO: STAGE The quartet finishes "Coney Island Babe." QUARTET (SINGING) Goodbye, my Coney Island Babe. The audience APPLAUDS enthusiastically. The quartet bows. A rock flies in from 0.S. and HITS Wiggum right between the eyes and knocks him out. Jailbird LAUGHS. 3) CHURCH The quartet SINGS while moving down the aisle with the collection baskets. The PARISHIONERS are throwing in fistfuls of money. Marge is there with little Bart and Lisa. ALL Goodbye, my Coney Island Baby/ Farewell, my own true love...

Homer leans into Marge. She gives a little SQUEAL.

At the altar, REVEREND LOVEJOY happily watches the money pour in. Flanders stands next to him in a choir robe.

REVEREND LOVEJOY

Ching, ching.

10.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

FLANDERS You know, Reverend, this really isn’t a hymn. REVEREND LOVEJOY (IRRITATED) Ned, would you go check the church marquee. I think some of the letters are loose. FLANDERS Oh my stars! He dashes out. INT. MOE’S TAVERN - DAY SCENE 6

The quartet SINGS the rest of "Coney Island Baby" to an enthusiastic CROWD.

HOMER (SINGS) So goodbye... WIGGUM (SINGS) Farewell... APU (SINGS) Shop Qwik-E-Mart and save... ALL Goodbye my Coney Isle/ Goodbye my Coney Isle/ Goodbye my Coney Island Babe! As the crowd CHEERS Homer steps up to the bar. Moe draws

him a beer. A flamboyant record producer, NIGEL, approaches Homer.

11.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92 12

NIGEL I got very excited watching you on that stage. I think I want to get behind you. (INTERESTED) Hmmm... HOMER I’m intrigued. NIGEL Is there someplace where we can be alone? HOMER How about the men’s room? NIGEL Delicious. INT. MOE’S TAVERN - MEN’S ROOM - MOMENTS LATER Homer and Nigel are alone. NIGEL Homer, I’m a theatrical agent, and I

want to represent your group.

HOMER Really?

NIGEL Yeah!

HOMER

We’re not the greatest singers in the

world.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

NIGEL I know that, but you’ve got something more important than talent. You’ve got... it! (DISTASTE) All except that police officer. (DISGUSTED NOISE) Too Village People. You’1ll have to replace him. HOMER But Chief Wiggum owns the pitch pipe! NIGEL With the money you’re going to make, you’ll be able to buy the Pitch Pipe Building! He points out the window to a hundred-story building topped by an enormous pitch pipe, which for some reason we’ve never seen before. INT. MOE’S TAVERN - BAR - MOMENTS LATER SCENE 7 Nigel meets the quartet. PRINCIPAL SKINNER (SHAKING HANDS) Principal Seymour Skinner. NIGEL That explains the blazer. Shudder. APU I am Apu Nahasapeemapetilon. NIGEL Never fit on a marquee, luv. From now

on, your name is Apu Du Beaumarchais.

13.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92 14.

APU It is a great dishonor to my ancestors and my God, but okay. HOMER Is there anything I should do different? NIGEL You don’t change a thing. Wiggum throws an arm around Homer’s shoulder. WIGGUM This is great. And the best part of it is I/ll be doing it with my pals. We’re going to the top boys... Together. HOMER (CLINKING GLASSES) Together!... There’s something else I’m supposed to mention... (REMEMBERS) Oh yeah. You’re out of the group. WIGGUM Oh I’m out of the... (SPIT TAKE) Ow! Right through the nose. INT. REHEARSAL HALL - DAY SCENE 8

Homer (with little Bart on his lap), Apu, and Skinner sit on folding chairs.

HOMER (V.0.) The next day, we started auditioning

for Chief Wiggum’s replacement...

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

AUDITION MONTAGE 1) GRAMPA wears a tux and SNAPS his fingers. GRAMPA Old McDonald had a farm/ Ee-i-ee-i-o/ And on this farm he had a chick/ Swingingest chick I know/ With a wiggle wiggle here and a wiggle wiggle there... HOMER Get off the stage! 2) GROUNDSKEEPER WILLIE SINGS. GROUNDSKEEPER WILLIE

When you’re alone and life is getting

you lonely, ye kin always go... Ach! Doontoon!

APU Next!

3) Chief Wiggum, wearing a fake mustache and top hat as a disguise, SINGS.

WIGGUM If I could walk with the animals/ talk with the animals/ grunt and squeak and squawk with the animals... His mustache falls off. PRINCIPAL SKINNER (SHOCKED) Good Lord! Dr. Doolittle

is Chief Wiggum!

15.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92 16.

WIGGUM This bird’s gonna fly! Wiggum jumps out the window.

4) JASPER SINGS.

JASPER Theeeeme... from "A Summer Place"... from "A Summer Place" ... the theme... from "A Summer Place"

It’s the theme... HOMER/SKINNER/ APU Next! INT. MOE’S TAVERN Homer, Skinner and Apu moodily stare at their drinks. SCN 9 HOMER (V.O.) One lousy applicant after another! It was like a scene out of "The Commitments," "The Fabulous Baker Boys," or "The Producers."

From the bathroom, they hear an astonishingly beautiful tenor.

TENOR (V.O.) (SINGING) Over in Killarney/ So many years ago...

APU Such a voice!

PRINCIPAL SKINNER

Who is that?

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

TENOR (SINGING) Me mither sang this song to me/ In tones so soft and (BELCH)... HOMER/SKINNER/ APU Barney! INT. MOE’S BATH ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Homer, Skinner and Apu BURST into the cruddy bathroom. Barney is crawling around on the floor.

BARNEY (SINGING) Just a little Irish ditty...

HOMER Barney, how’d you like to sing for our group?

BARNEY Sure, why not? Now where’s me toothpick.

INT. MOE’S TAVERN - NIGHT

Barney is wearing a straw boater, striped jacket, and bowtie. The others are dressed similarly.

ANGLE AUDIENCE The angry AUDIENCE holds signs reading: "Barney Stinks" and “Don’t Flip Our Wiggum." An effigy of Barney is burning in the background. CROWD (CHANTING) Wiggum forever, Barney

never! Wiggum forever, Barney

never!...

17.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

WIGGUM (CHUCKLES) Marge, looking worried, is there with little Bart and Lisa. BARNEY (SINGS) Swe-ee-ee-ee-eet Adeleine. HOMER/APU/ SKINNER (SINGS) Sweet Adeleine. Immediately, the crowd quiets down. BARNEY (SINGS) My-y-y-y-y~-y Adeleine. HOMER/APU/ SKINNER (SINGS) My Adeleine.... The girls SCREAM. CROWD Barney forever, Wiggum never! Barney forever, Wiggum never!.. At the bar, Nigel watches with Moe. MOE Hey, those girls you paid to screan are doing a great job. NIGEL I didn’t pay any girls to scream. MOE (TOO BIG) Hanh?!!! EXT. MOE’S TAVERN - 3:00 A.M. SCENE 10 Bow ties loosened and coats slung over their shoulders, the quartet triumphantly walks out of Moe’s and down the

deserted street, AD-LIBBING "What a nighel® "They wouldn’t let us leave the stage!" "Eleven encores!"

18

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

PRINCIPAL SKINNER Only one question remains gentlemen. What do we call ourselves? Nigel is feeling the crease of Homer’s jacket. NIGEL How about Handsome Homer Simpson Plus Three? BARNEY I like it. Skinner and Apu MUTTER disagreement. SKINNER We need a name that’s witty at first but that seems less funny each time you hear it. APU How about The Be Sharps! They all LAUGH. The LAUGH gets weaker and embarrassed. SKINNER Perfect. HOMER The Be Sharps. ANGLE ON THE HANDS Skinner places his hand on top. PRINCIPAL SKINNER (V.0O.) The Be Sharps. APU/BARNEY (V.0.)

The Be Sharps.

19

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

A slight beat, then. WIGGUM (V.0O.) The Be Sharps. (NERVOUS CHUCKLE) WIDEN OUT to reveal Wiggum. The others stare at him. WIGGUM Can’t blame a guy for trying. (NERVOUS CHUCKLE) You’re all under

arrest.

FADE OUT:

END OF ACT ONE

20.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

AcT TWO FADE IN: INT. SIMPSON CAR - PRESENT SCENE 11

The family drives home from the Swap Meet. The back seat has various junk in it.

HOMER What’d you kids get? Bart holds up a bong. BART I bought this cool pencil holder. HOMER Boy, are you stupid. (TO SELF) Doesn’t even know what a bong is. (LAUGHS) MARGE (SADLY) No one bought a wishbone necklace. HOMER Well, one of us made some money. I sold a guy our spare tire. SFX: TIRE BLOWOUT HOMER (ANNOYED GRUNT) EXT. HIGHWAY - EVENING Homer, Bart and Lisa sit by the side of the road on a

lonely stretch of desert highway. We see the top of Marge’s blue hair over the horizon.

21.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92 22.

HOMER (TO KIDS) It’ll be a long time before your Mother gets back with the tire, so why don’t I tell you more of the story? Now that we had a name, the Be Sharps needed a hit. Something new,

something exciting, something mid-

1980's... DISSOLVE TO: INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - DAY SCENE 12

Homer is noodling around on the piano trying to come up with a new song.

HOMER (SINGING) Thanks to Edison/ we have light bulbs/ Thanks to Heimlich we don’t choke/ But I’d really like to thank the guy/ who gave the world New Coke... HOMER (CONT’D) (TO SELF) Eh... None of those Edison songs are working. He CRUMPLES the sheet music and throws it onto one of three over-flowing waste baskets, which are overflowing with pages of crumpled sheet music.

Marge enters carrying baby Lisa. She holds up a "Baby on Board" sign.

MARGE Look what I got. Now people will

stop intentionally ramming our car.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

HOMER Hmm... baby on board. Homer turns back to the piano. HOMER (CONT’D) (SINGING) Baby on board/ something, something Burt Ward... (SPOKEN) This thing writes itself! INT. RECORDING STUDIO = NEXT DAY

The Be Sharps are gathered around the microphone, SINGING the end of Homer’s song.

BE SHARPS (SINGING) ... I’m telling you, it’s mighty nice/ each trip’s a trip to paradise... HOMER (SINGING) With my... BE SHARPS (SINGING) Ba-by on board. NIGEL Gentlemen, you’ve just recorded your first number one. The Be Sharps CHEER and exchange high-fives. HOMER

Wait’1l I tell Marge!

23.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

NIGEL (SOURLY) Oh yes... Aunt Bee Hive... I would prefer we kept your marriage a secret. You see, a lot of women are going to want to have sex with you, and we want them to think they can. HOMER (RELUCTANT) Well... if I explain it to Marge that way, I’m sure she’1l understand. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - DAY Marge SOBS uncontrollably. Homer tries to comfort her. HOMER Come on, honey. It’1l only be till we finish our tour of Sweden. INT. SPRINGFIELD RETIREMENT CASTLE - CONTINUOUS SCENE 13 Grampa listens to the song on his old Motorola. BE SHARPS (V.O) Baby on board/ how I’ve adored/ that sign on my car’s windowpane... GRAMPA That’s my boy Homer singing! Jasper pokes his head in. JASPER Paul Harvey’s on.

Grampa immediately spins the dial.

24

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

PAUL HARVEY (V.0.) and that little boy who nobody liked grew up to be... Roy Cohn. GRAMPA/JASPER Wow! PAUL HARVEY (V.O.) (CONT’D) And now you know the rest of the story. INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS The family is listening to the radio. BE SHARPS (V.O) . with my ba-by on board. MARGE Homer, you’re going to be famous! HOMER Yeah, but I’m not gonna let it change our lives. I’1ll be the same loving father I’ve always been. Marge looks around. MARGE Have you seen Bart? HOMER I stuck him somewhere. cuT TO: INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LAUNDRY ROOM - CONTINUOUS

Bart is imprisoned under a laundry basket with two heavy rocks on top of it.

25

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

EXT. KENNEDY AIRPORT = NEW YORK = DAY SCENE 14 SUPER: THE NEXT DAY

The airport is packed with thousands of SCREAMING out-of- control FANS as the Be Sharps’ plane lands. They wave to the crowd, like the Beatles in ‘64.

INT. AIRPORT - PRESS ROOM

Flashbulbs POP as the quartet takes questions from a mob of REPORTERS.

REPORTER #3 I have a question for Apu du Beaumarchais. Isn’t it true that you’re really an Indian? APU By the many arms of Vishnu, I swear it is a lie. REPORTER #1 Barney, how did you join the group? BARNEY They found me on the men’s room floor. The reporters LAUGH. CLOSE-UP BARNEY. A single tear rolls down his cheek. REPORTER #2 Principal Skinner... SKINNER

Mm hmm?

26.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

REPORTER #2 (CONT’D) You’ve been referred to as "The Funny One." Is that reputation justified? SKINNER Yes. Yes it is. More LAUGHTER. EXT. SPRINGFIELD RETIREMENT CASTLE - DAY Homer covers Grampa’s eyes as he leads him out to the curb. Parked there is a big pink Cadillac with a huge red bow on the hood. HOMER Look what I got for you, Dad. Homer removes his hands, revealing the Cadillac. GRAMPA Oh, Hom... (HEART ATTACK NOISES) gimme the keys. DISSOLVE TO: INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - NIGHT HOMER (V.O) The media crunch got so bad, I had to disguise myself so as not to attract attention. Homer enters the house in a horse costume. He is the front half. The costume opens to reveal Nigel is the back half of the horse. HOMER Thanks, Nigel. NIGEL

My pleasure.

27.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92 28.

INT. RECORD STORE = DAY SCENE 15 We see a life-size cardboard cut-out of the Be Sharps ina Springfield record store. The cut-out has them in leaping poses a la The Beatles on the "Hard Days Night" album. Marge is shopping with Bart and Lisa among the kiddy records. Nearby, two TEENAGE GIRLS moon over the cut-out. Marge eavesdrops. TEENAGE GIRL #1 I like Barney ‘cause I can tell he’s not gonna live very long. TEENAGE GIRL #2 And, Homer’s got the cutest (WHISPERS) . TEENAGE GIRL #1 I hear he had a butt-lift. MARGE (INDIGNANT) He did not! EXT. STATUE OF LIBERTY - NIGHT The Be Sharps perform on a stage. A banner reads: "Statue of Liberty Centennial." They SING special lyrics to "Swanee." BARNEY (SINGING) The grandest... BE SHARPS (BIG FINISH) ...face-lift we ever did see... The doff their boaters. BE SHARPS (CONT’D) Hats off to that big Green Lady!

The CROWD APPLAUDS. President Reagan smiles and CLAPS, then turns to Nancy.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

PRESIDENT REAGAN Damned ceremonies. This is time I could be working, Mommy. HOMER (COYLY) We’d like to dedicate this next number to a very special woman. She’s 100 years old, and she weighs over 200... (WINK) ... tons. PANICKY MAN This enormous woman will devour us all! (SCREAMS) He leaps over a railing. HOMER (POINTING) I meant the statue. INT. WIGGUM HOUSE = NIGHT ON TV

A white-haired talk show host is wearing a turban and holding an envelope to his head.

TALK SHOW HOST Okay...Gilligan, the Skipper and Chief Wiggum. (OPENS ENVELOPE) Name three castaways. SIDEKICK Hi-yo! They both LAUGH. BACK TO SCENE

Chief Wiggum GROWLS and SHOOTS several times at the TV with his revolver, missing it.

29.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

MRS. WIGGUM Clancy, use the remote. WIGGUM I can’t find it. MRS. WIGGUM Check your holster. WIGGUM Oh yeah.

He checks his holster and pulls out the remote. He changes the channel.

ON TV We see a gossipy FEMALE TALK SHOW HOST. FEMALE TALK SHOW HOST Can we talk about Chief Wiggum? (RETCHING SOUND) The talk show host sticks her finger down her throat. EXT. HIGHWAY - DAY - PRESENT SCENE 16

MARGE struggles to change the tire. Homer continues telling the story.

HOMER Soon, we were off again -- this time to the Grammy Awards... INT. PRIVATE JET - DAY Skinner, Apu and Barney sit up front, wearing sunglasses and Hawaiian shirts. They all have GROUPIES on their laps. Homer sits next to Nigel.

HOMER

I wish Marge was here.

30

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92 31.

NIGEL Must we talk about Bouffant Betty? HOMER How come you never want to talk about her? NIGEL You don’t want to know. HOMER Sure I do. NIGEL All right Homer. I’m very attracted to you, myself. Nigel arches one eyebrow impossibly high. HOMER (QUICKLY CHANGING SUBJECT) Uh huh. Hey, look at all those clouds out there. They all look like something, don’t they? That one looks like two men kissing. I mean, they’re professional ball players. There’s nothing funny going on. NIGEL Sob.

INT. CIVIC AUDITORIUM ~ NIGHT We see a curtain reading "29TH ANNUAL GRAMMY AWARDS."

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

HOMER (V.0) We had fame and fortune. Now all we needed was the approval of record company low-lifes. DAVID CROSBY is standing at the Grammy podium. DAVID CROSBY And the Grammy for Outstanding Soul, Spoken word, or Barbershop Album of the Year, goes to...The Be Sharps! Congratulations! The Be Sharps are at the podium, triumphantly waving fistfulls of Grammys. David Crosby stands behind them, smiling. BARNEY David Crosby, you’re my hero! DAVID CROSBY You like my music? BARNEY You’re a musician? INT. HOTEL SUITE - BEDROOM - NIGHT SCENE 17 Homer sits on the bed, still in his tuxedo with the tie loosened. His Grammy is on the nightstand. Homer is on the phone with Marge. HOMER So what was Lisa’s favorite present? INTERCUT INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM We see the remnants of Lisa’s birthday presents laying

around the room. A “HAPPY BIRTHDAY LISA" banner is on the wall.

32

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

HOMER (V.O) Did she like the Cadillac I sent her?

We see two-year-old Lisa on the floor TEARING pages out of the Cadillac owner’s manual and eating then.

MARGE She can’t get enough of it. Here, say hello to your birthday girl. HOMER Lisa, did you see the Grammys? TWO-YEAR-OLD LISA (INTO PHONE) You beat Falco! HOMER Well, he’1l be back. TWO-YEAR-OLD LISA I miss you, Daddy. HOMER I miss you too, honey.

Homer hangs up, picks up his Grammy and exits out onto the balcony.

EXT. HOTEL BALCONY - CONTINUOUS On the balcony, Homer looks out at the "Hollywood" sign. He looks over at a huge billboard showing a happy family with the slogan "Happy Family Brand Bourbon." HOMER (MOANS) A TEENAGE BELLBOY appears at this side. TEENAGE BELLBOY

Here’s the champagne you ordered, Mr.

Simpson.

HOMER

Oh. Thanks.

HOMER (CONT’D) Here.

SURLY MAN (0.S.) Hey! Don’t throw your garbage down here.

up an aerosol can with Homer’s face on it. She

HOMER They took the foam off the market because they found out it was poisonous. But if you ask me, if you’re dumb enough to eat it, you deserve to die. (LOOKING O.S., SHARPLY) Bart!

strings of funny foam hanging out of his mouth.

BART (INNOCENTLY) What?

popular.

35.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

BART What’d you do... screw up like The Beatles and say you’re bigger than Jesus?

HOMER All the time. It was the title of our second album!

He holds up an album called "Bigger Than Jesus!" Bart and Lisa exchange a look.

HOMER (CONT’D) But we were about to learn an iron law of show business: What goes up must come down. LISA What about Bob Hope? He’s been consistently popular for over 50 years. BART So has Sinatra. HOMER Well, anyway, we were all getting tired of -- LISA Dean Martin still packs ‘em in. BART Ditto Tom Jones. HOMER Shut up!

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

MARGE With your father on the road all the time I tried to compensate for his absence... INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY The kids are at the table. We see Marge has constructed a mock Homer with a picture of Homer as its head, a sack of potatoes for the gut, and balloon arms. She has a tape recorder in front of it and she presses the "play" button. HOMER (V.O.) Hi, kids... I love (SPEEDS UP UNINTELLIGIBLY) BART/LISA (SCREAM)

Marge runs up to fix the tape. She accidentally knocks “Homer’s" head off and one of his arms POPS.

BART/LISA (CONT’D) (SCREAM) HOMER (V.0.) Meanwhile, the group was having problems of its own... INT. RECORDING STUDIO - DAY SCENE 19 Homer, Skinner, and Apu stand around a microphone. HOMER/ SKINNER/APU (SING) For all the latest medical poop/ Call Surgeon General, C. Everett Koop/ Koop Koop Adoop APU

This tune is not happening.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

HOMER I’ve written a hit song or two, Du Beaumarchais. How many have you written?... And where’s Barney?

PRINCIPAL SKINNER Oh, he’s with his new girlfriend. (CONTEMPTUOUS) The Japanese conceptual artist.

Barney, sporting a beret, enters with his ORIENTAL GIRLFRIEND. They both wear black turtlenecks.

BARNEY Barbershop is in danger of growing stale. I’m taking it to strange new places.

He pops a cassette into a tape player. We hear an atonal Revolution Number 9 type song.

MAN IN LEDERHOSEN Yodel lay-hee, Yodel lay-hee, Yodelay-hee-e-e-e--. PRINCIPAL SKINNER These dangerous Alpine rhythms appeal right to the pelvis. INT. HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT The Be Sharps watch late-night TV. HOMER (V.0O.) Then, came the day we knew we were finished... ON TV SCENE 20 TALKSHOW HOST Mr. Howell, Ginger, and the Be Sharps. EXT. SPRINGFIELD ELEMENTARY SCHOOL - DAY Skinner returns to school PRINCIPAL SKINNER Ah, to return at last to the groves of academe. (TO 0.S. BOY) Get your mouth off of that drinking fountain, young man.

We see a RALPHISH BOY with his mouth stuck entirely around the drinking spigot.

RALPHISH BOY (MOUTH STUCK) It’s stuck! INT. QWIK-E-MART - DAY Apu puts on his apron. APU It may not be glamorous, but it’s good honest work. A WOMAN with a BABY walks up. WOMAN WITH BABY How much is this quart of milk? APU Twelve dollars. INT. MOE’S TAVERN - DAY Barney and Kako sit at the counter. MOE (FRIENDLY) Hey, Barney, what’1ll it be? BARNEY

I’d like a beer Moe.

40.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

KAKO I’d like a single plum floating in perfume, served in a man’s hat. MOE Here you go.

He immediately pulls out a beer and a plum floating in perfume, served in a brown derby.

INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - DAY Homer KNOCKS on the door. It opens and the kids run out. BART/LISA Daddy! They joyously wrap themselves around his legs. DISSOLVE TO: INT. HOMER AND MARGE’S BEDROOM - NIGHT

Homer and Marge are in their pajamas. A happy Marge lies next to Homer. Homer kisses her playfully.

HOMER

(SEDUCTIVELY) Marge, I missed you. MARGE

Homie... What if the kids hear? HOMER

Don‘t worry. They’re asleep.

PAN DOWN to Homer’s legs. Bart and Lisa are still wrapped around them, asleep.

DISSOLVE TO: INT. SIMPSON HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - THE PRESENT SCENE 21

Homer is on the couch with Bart and Lisa.

41.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

HOMER I’1l never forget my five-and-a-half weeks at the top.

BART Man, that’s some story.

LISA But there are still a few things I don’t get. Like how come we never heard about this until today?

BART Yeah, and what happened to the money you made?

LISA Why haven’t you hung up your gold record?

BART Since when could you write a song?

LISA Where’s my Cadillac?

HOMER (CHUCKLES) There are perfectly good answers to those questions, but they’11 have to wait for another night. Now off to bed.

The kids exit. Homer looks at the album thoughtfully, then picks up the phone.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

MOE’S TAVERN = ROOFTOP = NEXT DAY

Homer goes up on the roof of Moe’s Tavern. Skinner, Apu, and Barney are already there.

PRINCIPAL SKINNER (NOSTALGIC) I can’t remember the last time we were all together. APU Last year when we sang for Lawyer Aid. HOMER (PROUDLY) We raised fifty million dollars for corporate lawyers. BARNEY And a one and a two and a three... SKINNER Ba bum bum bun... BE SHARPS (SINGS) Baby on board/ how I’ve adored/ that sign on my car’s windowpane... ANGLE STREET Passers by turn and listen to the song. They point to the roof, in scenes reminiscent of the rooftop concert in "Let It Be." EXT. PITCH PIPE BLDG. We see a HUMAN FLY in a cape and colorful costume is climbing up the side of the building. A crowd has gathered

watching him. As the Be Sharps start to sing, the crowd turns and watches them.

43.

9F21 FINAL DELIVERY 10/30/92

HUMAN FLY (WIMPY VOICE) Hello... Human Fly here... C’mon. I stayed up all night dying my underwear. ANGLE ON PAPER BOY SCENE 22 He runs with a stack of papers under his arm. PAPER BOY Extra, extra! Be Sharps sing on rooftop! MAN What? Gimme one of those! He buys a paper and quickly looks through it. MAN (CONT’D) Wait a minute! There’s nothing in here about the Be Sharps. Come back here! He chases after the paper boy, who has run off. PAPER BOY Haw haw! ANGLE PAUL MCCARTNEY In a car. He looks out the window PAUL MCCARTNEY It’s been done. ANGLE - WIGGUM & LOU They’re listening from a parked patrol car. LOU

Pretty, eh, Chief?

44,
